Resolves #12789

Related: #12696
Related: #12788

Authored by GPT-5 (Codex Desktop). Session 720fc09a-e8ff-467d-b039-28120312964d.

Sharpens `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` after the #12788 merge so the Brain story no longer reads as "Neo added memory" or "the Body finally got a Brain." The release note now states the correct v12.1 baseline: Knowledge Base, Memory Core, GitHub Workflow, Neural Link, and session-summary recall already existed for a single agent. The v13 jump is the move from solo-agent continuity into a graph-backed, cross-family institution: Native Edge Graph database for DreamService, A2A messages, wakeups, stable identities, peer-readable reasoning, cross-family review, MX, and cloud-deployable Agent OS topology.

Evidence: L1 (source diff + static release-note audits + public repo/source boundary checks) -> L1 required (documentation-only #12789 ACs). No residuals.

## Deltas from Ticket

- Replaced the TL;DR with the corrected premise: v12.1 already had single-agent Agent OS memory; v13 makes that layer graph-backed, cross-family, wake-driven, and cloud-deployable.
- Corrected the 2026 industry contrast to distinguish harness instances/sessions from agents: Codex, Claude Desktop, Claude Code, and IDE copilots are harness surfaces; Neo agents are named maintainers powered by LLM families and coordinated through A2A/wake.
- Replaced the Body-heavy `The Engine Grew a Brain` opening with `The Agent OS Became an Institution`.
- Reframed the Brain chapter as `Memory Became Telepathy`, explicitly separating single-agent memory from multi-agent institutional memory.
- Added the v12.1 Memory Core boundary in the narrative body: Memory Core, semantic recall, and session summaries existed before v13; v13 made memory cross-agent, identity-backed, and cloud-transportable.
- Strengthened the 2026 market contrast without adding external vendor or funding claims to the release note. The public artifact says the single-agent memory category is infrastructure and positions Neo above it at the institution layer.
- Tightened the cloud chapter from "deployable onto yours" to tenant-scoped deployment topology: MCP servers, Chroma, Native Edge Graph, orchestrator, OIDC ingress, request context, Streamable HTTP / SSE, and public deployment guides.
- Linked the public Dream Pipeline guide and added a digest bullet for the v13 README identity reset.
- Removed the exact per-maintainer contribution-count column that Vega flagged as final-cut churn; kept the joined roster.
- Confidential/private deployment and strategy materials informed the need for this pass, but the release note and this PR cite only public repo surfaces and contain no private names, paths, deployment names, or operational details.
